item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fences are man-made objects, so I doubt it could hold him if he walks up to one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cant they just contain it in a natural environment with a fence around it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Considering some of his novels, I won't be too sure of that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, can we be really sure it is not Leo Tolstoy? Or his counterpart in the Foundation Universe?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I though Leo Tolstoy was a writer, but not an abnormality :/

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1440: Should you choose to challenge Death to a game of cards for your life, there is one thing you must never do.
Dr. ████: And what is that?
SCP-1440: Win.

Wait a moment… that's not even supposed to be a metaphor?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This dude beat the devil with cards?

…I'm pretty sure the way that this is meant to be read is that the man played a card game with the Brothers Death, and won.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I recognized it but I don't think it takes from the piece.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This dude beat the devil with cards? Damn, I'm gunna have to try that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's sad that no one is concerned.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a really famous Leo Tolstoy's photo, and I recognized him in an instant. How can you use it as an illustration? It ruins the credibility of the article completely. Despicable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
From the article

SCP-1440's anomalous nature becomes apparent once it comes into contact with human population or man-made objects and remains in contact with them for longer than a few days

While the next sentence does state "everything connected to humanity" this and the sentence after that both specifically refer to humans and things made by humans. So stuff that humans just interact with to a minimal degree wouldn't be affected. As the article also specifies humans, 1440 probably doesn't affect 1000, as they are noted to be a separate genus from Homo (though whether death gods care about taxonomy is up to debate).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This SCP is really really interesting and confusing. I get what its saying but what exactly defines a trace of humanity that defines what objects it can affect? If a human were to touch a does it get affected? If a human picked a bushes berries would the entire bush be affected? If we were to get scp-1000 for example and let it make something advanced and he went near it would it still be affected?

So many questions and yet it's so good!

item.title: Image Attribution
item.content:encoded:
Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leo_Tolstoy#/media/File:Leo_Tolstoy_1897,_black_and_white,_37767u.jpg

Public Domain

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this post because it so perfectly captures the idea of SCP with it's strange mystical origins, and it's interaction with the modern world.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a really nice scip. He cheated death and now death is mad at him but cant kill him, BECAUSE HE CHEATED DEATH. Here, take that upvote man, you earned it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly this, and the tales around it, are some of my favorites in this site. However I think there is a flaw that needs addressing.

SCP-1440 has at the point of the interview proven himself to be extremely dangerous and the researchers should at least have the jist of what his anomalous proterties are (especially due to how helpfull he is in questioning). Why would they try to contain him without taking any precautions? Why would they try to contain him at a fully staffed site? For that matter why would they bring him anywhere near something that could cause such a catastrophic disaster such as a Nuclear Reactor?

The foundation should not be so stupid to even try to contain him in a place where that many assests could be lost without any forsight.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm…maybe find a deep cave or something? There's a lot of natural formations that could be used to imprison someone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes this is the same folktale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This guy reminds me so much of tale of the old soldier that ended up being televised as a Jim Henson show. If memory serves, he always wins the game when he uses that particular deck of cards, the glass allows him to see Death when he looks through it above a sick person, and once he can see Death he can call him into the bag. He did that once but let Death free when he saw how much misery it caused to other humans, so now Death refuses to come near him. That's where the Jim Henson version ended, though, so I don't know who he cheated that causes harm to come to others as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Loved the article, just have some questions: How many degrees of separation from humanity are required to prevent 1440's effects? Could a robot (anomalous or non-anomalous) built by humans subsequently build a containment unit for him? What about something like trained animals, such as beavers or apes building a wooden containment unit? What is the range of his effect? Would a sufficiently large containment area allow him to keep his proximity to any individual structure or person limited to shorter periods of time?

If anyone has thoughts on this, I'd love to hear them.

item.title: Re: The 4 horseman
item.content:encoded: On the first part, points for interesting guess work, but check out SCP-1295. They're apparently fans of the coffee.

item.title: The 4 horseman
item.content:encoded: after reading both SCP-1440 and SCP-032 i noticed their connection which made me wonder what they were. After thinking for a while i realized that SCP-1440 and his brothers may be the 4 horseman and more specifically i think SCP-1440 is famine. 1 of his three brothers is indeed death and he explained that they all bring misfortune. What made me think he was famine is the effect SCP-032 has on non human related living things. While human related things around SCP-1440 are destroyed, SCP-032 effects the opposite on non-human related life but with an effect similar to causing famine as related to what SCP-1440 truly is. Non-human life forms that come in contact with SCP-032 experience an inability to produce energy, for plants an inability to photosynthesize and for animals issues with their digestive system causing them to starve. Given SCP-032 is presumably the recreation of SCP-1440s dead wife it is fair to assume that the choice of her effect on other beings would relate to him as well as his punishment. I would also like to propose the idea of a possible containment method for SCP-1440 with the rapid expansion of humanity. Would it be possible to create a network of tunnels underground spanning a great distance and length for him to walk indefinitely that would keep him away from the human population while also preventing contact with any 1 area for to long that it may cause its destruction?

item.title: Re: Photo ID
item.content:encoded:
I'm not sure what the copyright is on that,

From the page:

© Time Inc For personal non-commercial use only

So I think, unfortunately, this means we can't use it. Maybe the version of him that the Escape from Site-19 guys did could be substituted?

item.title: Re: Photo ID
item.content:encoded: I am fairly certain LIFE didn't do fake photo credits as an April fools joke in 1947. You may not be trolling, but honestly, you certainly seem to be making very random and unlikely claims for no discernible purpose.

item.title: Re: Photo ID
item.content:encoded:
Okay.

April 1rst btw :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The photo, and to some extent the article, really remind me of Leo Tolstoy, who spent his last years living ascetically and wandering Russia dressed as a peasant. He was also persecuted by the Russian government and the Russian Orthodox Church, so yeah, some interesting parallels, though I highly doubt he was the inspiration for this article.

I enjoyed this by the way.

item.title: Photo ID
item.content:encoded:
This is a photo of an old man in Stalingrad (now Volgograd), taken April 1, 1947, by Thomas D. MacAvoy, for LIFE Magazine.

http://images.google.com/hosted/life/b4e2e1c15f35828e.html

I'm not sure what the copyright is on that, but I'm pretty sure those details mean it's not Yosef Schneersohn.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Alright, well. SOMEONE CREATE THAT GUIDE! :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not at all Yossi, Not at all. That's why I said that I do not recognize the man, maybe others will.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Are you chabadnic BOXER9999?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As a Jew I have to say that I do not recognize the man. I think that since both 1440 and the Rabbi come from eastern europe and both are old men it is probable that both have a similar apearance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I am familiar with the man, I am not at all certain this is indeed a picture of him. It does not match other images of him I have found, such as this one:

http://w3.chabad.org/media/images/3610.jpg

The shape of the nose in particular seems quite dissimilar. Additionally, the outfit presented in this image does not appear to fit that which is typically wore by Chabbad-associated rabbis.

If you have any clear indication this image is indeed of the 6th Rebbe, I'd be happy to see it, though I'm unlikely to change it regardless. As people said, it's a good image, and since you're the first one to make this claim in years, I think he's just obscure enough for me to keep it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think Dmatix is actually a member of the community that would recognize the picture. Like, I'm fairly certain he's a native Israeli, and Judaism is kind of a big thing in Israel. (Dmatix or anyone who knows him can correct me if I'm, like, dead wrong.)

Granted, using a picture of a fairly well-known person might be a bad idea, but a good picture that's CC-compliant is a good picture that's CC-compliant. If you want a picture that badly, you take what you can get.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…

I'm not even kidding…

No trolls here.

EDIT:
Yea, that would actually be a good Idea as a guide. Look up a photo before you use it because said person might be well known in a community.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Edit: You know what, this is just way off topic, and I think you are maybe trolling. If you seriously have never heard of the Pope or WWII, maybe you should start a thread about using photos of famous people in skips, I will be happy to follow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wasn't joking. I honestly never even heard that name until now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Pope is the head priest of the Roman Catholic Church, which is one of the largest religions in the world, so I imagine you are joking or something. Pius XII was Pope during the Second World War which was a pretty big historical event, that you probably also have heard about, and he also met with several US Presidents (and other significant world leaders including the fascist dictators).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, he is, just he's not famous in Christianity. He's famous in Judaism. Also, who is Pope Pius XII?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't think he is as well known as you seem to think he his. I have never heard of him personally. He certainly isn't more well known than Pope Pius XII, or do you also not like that picture?

I suspect the author chose the picture independent of the subject's identity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yea, but why choose a largely known religious figure? I mean, he met 3 separate presidents of the USA!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Because it's a great picture of an old man with a lot of character and a sack?

Every picture of a fictional someone here is in fact a picture of some real person…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Quick Question: Why is SCP-1440's picture a picture of the 6th Lubabitcher Rebbe? (From Chabad Judaism)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yep, I love the old man too.

item.title: Re: Contact?
item.content:encoded: Didn't read this one yet. Thanks!

item.title: Re: Contact?
item.content:encoded: I believe that some of your questions can also be answered by reading the tale Thrice, which is fully in-canon with this article.

item.title: Re: Contact?
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for the reply.

If I was an SCP researcher I would have tried some things:
a) see if observing SCP-1440 from a distance of X has an effect on the observer after Y days. I'm sure SC-1440 would comply with staying in the same natural location for the requierd amount of time.
b) contain SCP-1440 within a natural cave where the only entrance is a vertical shaft that cannot be climbed. A containment wall could be build around the entrance to this cave at a radial distance where it is no longer in contact with SCP-1440.

I'm sure SCP-1440 has some way around these things due to the ambiguity of "contact" (perhaps seeing SCP-1440, or being acknowledged as present by SCP-1440 is enough to satisfy conditions) but these are the things I thought of when I read it and was dissapointed that they hadn't been noted or tried by researchers as they had 7 opportunities to do so.

Still, love the article! :)

item.title: Re: Contact?
item.content:encoded: Relative proximity for the most part, but due to the capricious nature of the entities responsible for 1440's condition, there is no clear cut condition for when the effects begin to take place. The Foundation keeps the language vague here simply because they themselves don't truly understand 1440's triggering mechanism. This is one of the things that make it as dangerous an SCP as it is.

item.title: Contact?
item.content:encoded:
First: Loved the article.

Second: A question. The article states:
"SCP-1440's anomalous nature becomes apparent once it comes into contact with human population or man-made objects and remains in contact with them for longer than a few days"

What does it mean by contact? Is it proximity? Sight? It obviously isn't literal contact.

Thanks!
weeeeee

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "The piece was inspired by several old folktales, most notably the one about the soldier who caught death in a sack. There is a great version of that one on the TV show The Storyteller." thats exactly what it reminded me of ^^ your inspiration is well founded
Another one hundred votes, another milestone. I'm glad to see this one made it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And… why would that do anything? The only things he doesn't destroy are the possessions on his person, and those specific possessions at that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You could give him the deed to the land you're containing him in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's explored in Thrice, which I pretty much consider a canonical part of the article. The short of it is, the Three Brothers would make sure he got out. He tried throwing himself into holes in the past, but they just bring him back up. This is all about them tormenting him, after all, and they won't give up that easily.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would it not be possible to toss SCP-1440 in a hole? If they find a naturally occurring cave or something with no way for him to climb out, he'll be trapped there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure why but I feel like this has Nordic or Norwegian origins… Maybe it's the picture.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd actually argue that given the guy's abilities the Foundation would have no trouble keeping him a secret. He's a guy wandering around the world, and any humans or man made objects around him will begin to break down and cause destruction if he stays there too long. So think about it. It's not just, "Boom." everyone dies by some unexplained monster or explosion, but from the interview it sounds like he causes things to go wrong. Things could easily be explained when if a gas line exploded, or a ship sank, it was caused by him, because it would look natural, if not unexpected.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To a certain extent I think his condition is his containment, what with the "people dying around him". Now, it might take a few additional deaths to contain that, but, you know, greater good and so forth.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is another one of those articles that isn't necessarily bad, but breaks believability too much for me. Given this guy's abilities, I simply can't see any way the Foundation could keep his existence a secret. -1, sorry.
